perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, HICO and ______________ desire to exchange certain Confidential Information (as defined below) for the purpose of internal review in relation to the consideration of a potential business relationship or transaction (the “Purpose” );
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, HICO and ______________ desire to exchange certain Confidential Information (as defined below) for the purpose of internal review in relation to the consideration of a potential business partnership or transaction (the “Objective” );
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Purpose" to "Objective" in the definition creates confusion. Later sections refer to the 'Purpose' of the agreement, making it unclear if 'Objective' is now the correct term. This creates an in-text contradiction by altering a key term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereto agrees not to use the Confidential Information received by such party as a Receiving Party other than for the Purpose of this Agreement. Each party hereto shall: (a) not disclose the Confidential Information of the other party hereto to any other party and will use best efforts to protect the confidentiality of such information, (b) not use the Confidential Information except for the Purpose of this Agreement, and (c) not {J1872881.2} 2 reproduce, copy or photograph any document or other medium which contains Confidential Information, without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party; provided, however, that each party hereto may furnish the other’s Confidential Information to those employees or representatives of such party who need to have access to such Confidential Information to assist such party in achieving the Purpose of this Agreement. As a condition to such disclosure, the Receiving Party must inform its employees and representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and the terms of this Agreement and each representative of a Receiving Party that receives Confidential Information must agree in writing to be bound by the terms of this Agreement as is the Receiving Party. Each party hereto is responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its employees and representatives. The Receiving Party agrees to promptly advise the Disclosing Party in writing upon learning of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Purpose' for the agreement's objective. The changed text replaces 'Purpose' with 'Objective' in that initial definition. However, the contradicted text repeatedly uses 'Purpose' as the operative term. This creates a contradiction because the contract now refers to two different terms ('Purpose' and 'Objective') for the same concept (the reason for the agreement), making it unclear which term is legally binding and creating inconsistency regarding the application of the Confidential Information. As defined, misaligned terminology leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. The explanation provided further supports that there is indeed an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information does not include information that (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party, (b) was in the Receiving Party’s possession free from any obligation of confidence at the time it was communicated to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party, (c) becomes available to the Receiving Party on a non- confidential basis from a source other than the Disclosing Party, provided that such source is not prohibited from transmitting such info rmation by a contractual, legal, or other obligation, or (d) it was developed by employees or agents of the Receiving Party independently of and without reference to any information communicated to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Protected Information does not include information that (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party, (b) was in the Receiving Party’s possession free from any obligation of confidence at the time it was communicated to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party, (c) becomes available to the Receiving Party on a non- confidential basis from a source other than the Disclosing Party, provided that such source is not prohibited from transmitting such info rmation by a contractual, legal, or other obligation, or (d) it was developed by employees or agents of the Receiving Party independently of and without reference to any information communicated to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' creates ambiguity. The agreement repeatedly references 'Confidential Information,' so it's unclear if 'Protected Information' is now a synonym or something different, particularly given that 'Confidential Information' is specifically defined, while 'Protected Information' is not, contradicting its usage. Now creating ambiguity on what the parties are protecting.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereto agrees not to use the Confidential Information received by such party as a Receiving Party other than for the Purpose of this Agreement. Each party hereto shall: (a) not disclose the Confidential Information of the other party hereto to any other party and will use best efforts to protect the confidentiality of such information, (b) not use the Confidential Information except for the Purpose of this Agreement, and (c) not {J1872881.2} 2 reproduce, copy or photograph any document or other medium which contains Confidential Information, without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party; provided, however, that each party hereto may furnish the other’s Confidential Information to those employees or representatives of such party who need to have access to such Confidential Information to assist such party in achieving the Purpose of this Agreement. As a condition to such disclosure, the Receiving Party must inform its employees and representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and the terms of this Agreement and each representative of a Receiving Party that receives Confidential Information must agree in writing to be bound by the terms of this Agreement as is the Receiving Party. Each party hereto is responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its employees and representatives. The Receiving Party agrees to promptly advise the Disclosing Party in writing upon learning of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' introduces ambiguity and creates a contradiction. The 'Contradicted Text' section explicitly and repeatedly refers to 'Confidential Information' and outlines obligations related to its use and protection. Because of the modification, it is now ambiguous if 'Protected Information' is intended to be the same as 'Confidential Information,' or if it is a different type of information subject to different rules. This ambiguity directly contradicts the original intent and creates uncertainty about which information the obligations in the 'Contradicted Text' apply to, potentially rendering the original agreement unenforceable with respect to confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Each party hereto is responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its employees and representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party hereto is responsible for any violation of this Agreement by its employees and representatives.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'breach' to 'violation' subtly alters the legal weight of non-compliance. While seemingly similar, 'breach' often implies a more significant failure to perform, while 'violation' can encompass minor infractions. It creates an in-text contradiction by using two terms interchangeably that might have differing legal implications, mainly when enforcing this NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereto acknowledges that remedies at law may be inadequate to protect the other party against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement, and, without prejudice to any other rights and remedies otherwise available to t he parties hereto, agrees to the granting of injunctive relief in favor of the other without proof of irreparable harm or the posting of any bond. In the event of litigation commenced by a party to enforce this Agreement, the primarily prevailing party will be entitled to recover its costs and expenses in such litigation, including attorneys’ fees and expenses.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'breach' to 'violation' introduces a misaligned terminology contradiction. While the original text consistently refers to 'breach' in the contradicted section, the changed text introduces 'violation.' As the explanation states, breach and violation have different implications. Specifically, the contradicted text discusses remedies, injunctive relief, and attorney's fees in the event of a 'breach,' but the introduction of 'violation' elsewhere in the agreement creates uncertainty as to whether those same remedies apply to mere 'violations' as well, making it unclear whether the remedies apply to only 'breaches' or both 'breaches' and 'violations.' Therefore, the modification introduces a strong contradiction according to the misaligned terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information is and will remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party, and no right or license is granted to Receiving Party with respect to any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: All Secret Information is and will remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party, and no right or license is granted to Receiving Party with respect to any Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Switching 'Confidential Information' to 'Secret Information' in this clause creates an in-text contradiction, considering the contract only defines 'Confidential Information'. It's now unclear what exactly is considered the property of the disclosing party and what rights the receiving party has, particularly if 'Secret Information' has a broader or narrower scope than 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Secret Information is and will remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party, and no right or license is granted to Receiving Party with respect to any Confidential Information. Upon the termination by ei ther party of discussions concerning the Purpose of this Agreement, or sooner if so requested by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Confidential Information, including all copies of the same. Upon request, the fact of any such destruction must be certified in writing to the Disclosing Party by an officer of the Receiving Party. Nothing in this Agreement obligates either party hereto to disclose any information to the other or creates any agency or partnership relation between them.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Secret Information'. The contradicted text references 'Confidential Information', which is defined in the original agreement, but not 'Secret Information'. Since the changed text now uses 'Secret Information', the contradicted text which is referencing 'Confidential Information' now creates a contradiction, leading to uncertainty in enforcement regarding the obligations and rights of each party, because it is unclear whether 'Secret Information' and 'Confidential Information' are the same or different. This adheres to the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the termination by ei ther party of discussions concerning the Purpose of this Agreement, or sooner if so requested by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Confidential Information, including all copies of the same.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the termination by ei ther party of discussions concerning the Aim of this Agreement, or sooner if so requested by the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party will return to the Disclosing Party or destroy all Confidential Information, including all copies of the same.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the word 'Purpose' to 'Aim' creates confusion within the context of the entire document. As other sections explicitly refer to the 'Purpose,' this substitution introduces an ambiguity. It becomes unclear whether 'Aim' is now a synonym for 'Purpose,' or if it represents a separate, undefined concept, impacting the Receiving Party's obligations upon termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereto agrees not to use the Confidential Information received by such party as a Receiving Party other than for the Purpose of this Agreement. Each party hereto shall: (a) not disclose the Confidential Information of the other party hereto to any other party and will use best efforts to protect the confidentiality of such information, (b) not use the Confidential Information except for the Purpose of this Agreement, and (c) not {J1872881.2} 2 reproduce, copy or photograph any document or other medium which contains Confidential Information, without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party; provided, however, that each party hereto may furnish the other’s Confidential Information to those employees or representatives of such party who need to have access to such Confidential Information to assist such party in achieving the Purpose of this Agreement. As a condition to such disclosure, the Receiving Party must inform its employees and representatives of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and the terms of this Agreement and each representative of a Receiving Party that receives Confidential Information must agree in writing to be bound by the terms of this Agreement as is the Receiving Party. Each party hereto is responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its employees and representatives. The Receiving Party agrees to promptly advise the Disclosing Party in writing upon learning of any unauthorized use or disclosure of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Purpose' to 'Aim' in the Changed Text creates a strong in-text contradiction with the Contradicted Text. The Contradicted Text repeatedly and explicitly states that Confidential Information can only be used 'for the Purpose of this Agreement.' By introducing 'Aim' as a possible alternative to 'Purpose' upon termination, the document becomes ambiguous. It is unclear whether obligations related to the 'Purpose' also apply to the 'Aim,' or if 'Aim' refers to a different, undefined concept. This ambiguity directly impacts the Receiving Party's obligations regarding the use and return of Confidential Information, creating an enforceable conflict as per the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: If either party here to, as Receiving Party, is requested or required by legal or administrative process to disclose any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, such Receiving Party will promptly notify the Disclosing Party of such request or requirement so that such Disclosing Party may seek an appropriate protective order or other relief.
perturbation.changed_text: If either party here to, as Receiving Party, is requested or required by legal or administrative process to disclose any Private Information of the Disclosing Party, such Receiving Party will promptly notify the Disclosing Party of such request or requirement so that such Disclosing Party may seek an appropriate protective order or other relief.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Private Information' without definition creates an in-text contradiction. The agreement only defines 'Confidential Information', so replacing it with 'Private Information' introduces uncertainty about the scope of information that must be protected and disclosed under legal processes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Confidential Information” as used in this Agreement means all information regarding a Disclosing Party’s business disclosed or made available to a Receiving Party by a Disclosing Party, or otherwise acquired by Rece iving Party, in connection with the Purpose of this Agreement, including, without limitation, any information relating to a Disclosing Party’s business, products, technology, data, engineering data or drawings, software (including all algorithms, methods, techniques and processes revealed by such software), customers, customer lists, customer needs and requirements, documentation, training materials, vendors, intellectual property, marketing, financial, projections, trade secrets, proprietary information or other information, whether in electronic, oral or written form, and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party which contain or reflect such information. The existence of the Purpose of this Agreement, this Agreement and the terms contained herein shall be de emed to be Confidential Information under this Agreement. Confidential Information does not include information that (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party, (b) was in the Receiving Party’s possession free from any obligation of confidence at the time it was communicated to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party, (c) becomes available to the Receiving Party on a non- confidential basis from a source other than the Disclosing Party, provided that such source is not prohibited from transmitting such info rmation by a contractual, legal, or other obligation, or (d) it was developed by employees or agents of the Receiving Party independently of and without reference to any information communicated to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', which is defined in the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Private Information'. Because 'Private Information' is not defined in the agreement, and 'Confidential Information' is explicitly defined, this creates a direct contradiction because the scope of protected information becomes ambiguous. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', as it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.
